checks.a_analysis: The JSON output contains only text found in the source document. No fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or headings were introduced by the conversion pipeline.
The source document contains no contact information such as phone numbers or URLs, and none were present in the output JSON.
All thematic sections ('Class Concept', 'Recruitment Standards', etc.) from the single-page source document are present in the JSON. No sections or pages were entirely omitted.
The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.a_analysis: The main body text for all sections has been preserved in full within the JSON elements. There are no substantial omissions within any section.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Identifying metadata from the document header is missing in the output. Specifically, the jurisdiction ('State of North Carolina'), the classification code ('32000018'), and the revision date ('06/2018') appear in the source header but are not captured in the JSON content or metadata fields.
checks.b_evidence_output: [Content missing from JSON elements and metadata fields]
checks.b_evidence_location: Header (top left and top right of source document)

checks.a_analysis: I have carefully compared the source PDF with the JSON output. There are no instances of duplicated content. Each section and paragraph from the source is represented exactly once in the output elements.

The output uses H2 for all major sections: 'Class Concept', 'Recruitment Standards', 'Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities', and 'Minimum Education and Experience'. In the source, 'Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities' is visually a subsection of 'Recruitment Standards'. While the hierarchy has been flattened to H2, there are no skips in heading levels (e.g., H1 to H3), and the normalization for screen reader navigation is within the acceptable changes parameters.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no table structure corruption can occur.

The main body text follows the logical reading order of the source document. The disclaimer note ('Note: This is a generalized representation...'), which appears at the bottom of the source PDF, has been moved to the top of the JSON sections. This is an acceptable reordering for accessibility purposes, ensuring disclaimers are presented to users before the main content.

The bulleted list under 'Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities' has been correctly identified and converted into an unordered list structure in the JSON. The number of items and the content of each item accurately reflect the source document.
The source document contains no hyperlinks or contact information, and none have been fabricated or injected into the JSON output.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document is faithfully maintained. While the HTML version introduces a table of contents sidebar and moves the footer note about ADA functions to the top of the main content area, these are standard adjustments for accessible web design. The sequential flow of information (Class Concept followed by Recruitment Standards) matches the source PDF. The layout is clean and the hierarchy of information is clear.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no table rendering issues exist.
The source document is entirely text-based and does not contain any meaningful images or logos to be rendered in the HTML.

There is no visual duplication of body content. The appearance of section titles in the navigation sidebar as well as the main body is an expected feature of accessible navigation landmarks and does not constitute content duplication.
